purpose: Map the workload region where compressed paged attention reaches latency parity with an equally optimized dense-f16 kernel.

initial_sweep.finding: A marginal 1.02–1.04× latency win appears at head_dim 64 from about 16K context onward. No crossover appears at head_dim 128 through 64K.
initial_sweep.primary_value: The decisive value remains 2.67× more context at equal VRAM, not broad latency acceleration.

parity_rule: The median compressed latency must be no more than 2% slower than the median fair dense latency, with correctness passing and both coefficients of variation at or below 5%.
win_rule: The median compressed latency must be at least 2% faster than the median fair dense latency, with lower KV memory, correctness passing, and both coefficients of variation at or below 5%.
fairness: Dense and compressed kernels must use the same warp decomposition, online-softmax algorithm, query layout, causal window, launch geometry class and timing method.
Only the storage/read representation may differ: raw f16 for dense and fixed-width compressed decode for ChromoFold.
Warmup count, measured iterations, clocks and power state must be recorded.
Negative and no-crossover results must be retained.
boundary: The located crossover is an engine-level finding on one GPU and a narrow workload region. It does not establish a universal latency win or an end-to-end serving win until the live runtime uses the compressed KV cache.
